title: Counting Bits
problem_statement: Given a non-negative integer n, return an array of length n + 1 where each element is the number of 1 bits in the binary representation of its index.

examples.explanation: 0 has 0 ones, 1 has 1 one, and 2 (10) has 1 one.

intuition: Build the answer incrementally by using previously computed bit counts and the lowest set bit property.

approaches.name: Dynamic programming with bit tricks
approaches.description: Use the relation countBits[i] = countBits[i & (i-1)] + 1 to compute counts efficiently.
approaches.why_not: Counting bits for each number separately is slower at O(n log n).

interview_qa.q: What trick speeds up counting bits?
interview_qa.a: Use the operation i & (i-1) to remove the lowest set bit and reuse previous results.
Each number is processed once, and the bit trick reduces the work per number.
